name: MrBang21
content: In Nordic and broader European medical standards, infant circumcision is not a medically indicated procedure. A healthy newborn male does not require removal of foreskin tissue, and being born with a penis is not a medical indication for surgery.

[Infant circumcision](https://mrbang21.github.io/human/#male_circ_infant) is a non-therapeutic surgical intervention performed on healthy tissue in a non-consenting child. Many European medical perspectives describe routine infant circumcision as unnecessary, with some Nordic medical discourse explicitly classifying it as genital mutilation.
I live in Denmark, where [circumcision](https://mrbang21.github.io/human/#male_circumcision) is rare outside specific religious contexts and is not part of standard pediatric practice.

Some circumcised men report [loss of sensation](https://mrbang21.github.io/human/#male_circ_loss_of_sensitivity), altered sexual experience, or psychological distress, while others report neutrality or acceptance.
This collection exists to document lived experiences of circumcision without institutional or ideological filtering.
Every testimony belongs to a real person with a unique perspective. Some were circumcised as infants, others later in life, and some are partners or family members affected indirectly.
**Submit Your Own Testimony**
If circumcision has affected you or someone close to you, you can share your story.
You can contact me through [@MrBang21](https://x.com/MrBang21).
Submissions can be any length and may include personal experience, reflections, or views on infant circumcision and genital autonomy.

**Privacy Options**
You choose how your testimony is published:

***Option 1: Completely Anonymous***
No name, profile, or identifying information will be shown.

***Option 2: Named***
A name or nickname will be displayed.

***Option 3: Linked Profile***
A link to your social media profile can be included if you choose.
Country or general location (for example Denmark, Germany, United States) may be included if you want contextual background while remaining otherwise anonymous.
No testimony is published without explicit consent.
*"Every testimony reflects a lived reality. Every lived reality deserves to be recorded with honesty."*

name: Nikorasu85
content: I complain!
I will always wish it were not done to me.
It caused years of confusion. I now know what I'm missing and I will never get to experience what an uncut man gets to.
Someone else got to decide how much sensation and pleasure I get to feel in life.

name: Fokam Kuate Hervé
content: Male genital mutilation (circumcision) is legal here. Female genital mutilation is illegal.
I did not realize I had been circumcised until I was 30 years old. Discovering that a part of my body had been permanently removed without my knowledge or consent was shocking.

When I confronted my parents, they gave me every justification imaginable. Tradition. Religion. HIV prevention. Claims that women prefer it. Even accusations that I had been "brainwashed" by France. Excuse after excuse, anything except acknowledging what had been done to me.
I demanded one of two things: a sincere apology or that I would remove them from my life. They knelt and asked for forgiveness, and for a time I believed they genuinely regretted their decision.
Later, I realized nothing had really changed.

Whenever I tried to discuss circumcision, they ignored me. They allowed their financially dependent son to have their grandchildren circumcised. They continued associating with relatives who tried to silence me whenever I spoke about the issue.
Their actions showed me that they did not truly regret what they had done. They were comfortable maintaining relationships with people who supported and perpetuated the same practice.
The hardest part was not discovering that I had been circumcised. The hardest part was realizing how little concern the people around me had for what had been taken from me.
That realization changed how I viewed my family. Eventually, I chose to cut ties and disappear from their lives. Today, none of my relatives know where I live.

My experience taught me that circumcision is not only about the physical act itself. It is also about the refusal to listen to those who object to what was done to them, even after they find the courage to speak.

name: Drew Walker
content: I was circumsized when I was an infant. I learned about the functions of the foreskin when I was a teenager and confronted my father about his decision to mutilate my body. He brushed it off and said it was done to him as well. This is ritualistic abuse done to so many children for no legitimate reason. I don’t care about someone‘s religion. If your religion compells you to do this to infants or any man then you‘re a monster. If you have this done to your children because it was done to you then you are a coward. I have been restoring my foreskin, and it is a long, tiring process. I want everyone responsible for this practice to hang. I want to see a world where circumsision is eradicated.

name: Tim K
content: I was indifferent to it into early adulthood. I was lightly exposed to what it entails via **Penn & Teller:Bullshit!** My wife joined me in that opposition when she saw how lethargic and damaged infants are after being cut.

My real turning point was being re-introduced to the idea of "restoring". (Bullshit did cover that, but I saw it as a weird thing old men do at the time.) I looked into it for a solid week before starting.
Those early results made things better and I started checking both sides of the circumcision debate.
The more function of foreskin I regained, the better things were for me. I realized I should've had what I gained, and more, for my whole life.
So should others. I also delved more into the risks vs benefits and saw how weak benefits were and how prevalent the damages are.

name: Dare Win
content: I was a young boy when the scar first peeled. I didn't understand what had happened, but I knew instinctively that I didn't like it.
